title: Implement the scanner pre-filter with slither and semgrep parsers
description: Deterministic ground truth for the LLM auditor: a SecurityScannerPreFilter that runs config.security.scanners commands under the shared audit AbortSignal with per-scanner isolation (missing binary → [], hang → SIGKILL), dedicated parsers mapping slither JSON and semgrep JSON output to SecurityFinding[] (fixture-tested, binaries NOT required in CI), a raw-text fallback for unknown scanners, and wiring of the resulting priors into runSecurityAudit's prompt (ADR-4: absent scanners config = pure LLM pass, zero child processes).

successCriteria.description: The slither parser maps a committed slither --json output fixture to the expected SecurityFinding[] (path, line, severity bucket, vulnClass, description) and the semgrep parser does the same for a committed semgrep --json fixture; both are pure functions with malformed-input tests returning [] (never throwing).

runScannerPreFilter executes each configured scanner command via execa with per-scanner isolation: a missing binary (ENOENT) or nonzero exit yields [] for that scanner while other scanners still run and contribute; the function never rejects.
When the shared AbortSignal fires mid-scan, scanner child processes are killed (SIGKILL/forceKill semantics) and the pre-filter resolves with the findings gathered so far (partial results, no hang).

Parser selection is name/command-based (slither→slither parser, semgrep→semgrep parser, anything else→raw-text-excerpt fallback finding priors), and scanner priors appear in the auditor prompt under a deterministic-ground-truth section with scannerRan:true on the result; with scanners:[] no child process is spawned, scannerRan is false, and the prompt has no priors section (byte-identical to sprint 2 behavior).
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass; ESLint zero errors; full suite green — and no test requires slither or semgrep binaries to be installed (fixtures only).

nonGoals: Do not add parsers beyond slither and semgrep (raw-text fallback covers the rest).
Do not let scanner findings bypass the LLM auditor or directly drive the verdict — they are priors; the audit's review buckets remain the verdict source.
Do not install or vendor scanner binaries; do not add npm dependencies (execa already exists).
Do not change the gate or CLI (they inherit priors through the core).
stopConditions: All five criteria verified; CI-offline property confirmed (suite passes on a machine without slither/semgrep).
definitionOfDone: Operators can configure scanner commands per project; slither/semgrep output arrives as typed, fixture-verified SecurityFinding priors in the auditor prompt under the shared time-box, unknown scanners degrade to raw-text priors, and unconfigured audits remain pure LLM passes with zero child processes.
assumptions: Scanner entries reuse the EvalStrategy shape's command field (config.security.scanners: EvalStrategySchema[] from sprint 1, per ADR-4); the scanner 'name' for parser selection derives from the strategy's name/command string (e.g. command starts with or contains 'slither'/'semgrep').
execa (already a dependency) supports signal-based cancellation/forceKill for sc-5-3; child stdout is bounded (maxBuffer or truncation) so a pathological scanner cannot exhaust memory.

Slither fixture shape: results.detectors[] with elements[].source_mapping (filename_relative, lines[]), impact (High/Medium/Low), check id, description. Semgrep fixture shape: results[] with path, start.line, extra.severity (ERROR/WARNING), check_id, extra.message. Severity mapping: slither High-impact and semgrep ERROR → critical bucket priors; lower severities → important. The generator verifies these shapes against real tool docs/output when writing fixtures and documents the mapping in code.
Priors are prompt-side only: sprint 2's runSecurityAudit already accepts priors?: SecurityFinding[] (or gains it now with a default that keeps existing tests green).
outOfScope: Hub emission (sprint 6), skill/dogfood/docs (sprint 7), any additional scanner parsers, dependency-vulnerability audit wiring (npm audit etc.).
generatorNotes: Anchors may have drifted — re-locate by pattern. (1) src/orchestrator/security-scanners.ts: export runScannerPreFilter({scanners, projectRoot, signal}) plus pure parseSlitherOutput(json: unknown) and parseSemgrepOutput(json: unknown) — parse with defensive narrowing (unknown → shape checks), NEVER trust fixture shape at runtime; malformed → []. Map vulnClass heuristically from check ids (e.g. slither 'reentrancy-*' → leave vulnClass undefined unless a clean mapping exists — vulnClass is optional; do not force wrong classes). (2) execa usage: follow existing execa call sites (fleet spawner, eval strategies) for options style; pass {reject:false} or try/catch per scanner; wire signal via execa's cancelSignal/signal option and forceKillAfterDelay for sc-5-3. Cap stdout (maxBuffer) and truncate raw-text fallback priors to a bounded excerpt (~2000 chars). (3) Fixtures: commit realistic minimal slither/semgrep JSON files under a __fixtures__ dir next to the tests (grep for how medical MedlinePlus fixtures are laid out and follow that convention). (4) Wire into runSecurityAudit: when config.security.scanners is non-empty, call the pre-filter INSIDE the audit path (so the gate's existing Promise.race time-box covers it) before building the prompt; pass an AbortSignal derived from the same timeout — check how sprint 3's gate structured the race and thread the signal (if the gate races an un-abortable promise today, create the AbortController in runSecurityAudit keyed to security.timeoutMs and pass its signal to the pre-filter). (5) For the abort test: use a fake long-running command (node -e with a sleep) rather than a real scanner — keep it fast with a short signal fire.
evaluatorNotes: For sc-5-1: verify fixtures look like REAL tool output (spot-check field names against slither/semgrep documentation — a fixture invented to match the parser is circular); malformed-input tests must include truncated JSON and a valid-JSON-wrong-shape case. For sc-5-2: three-scanner test where the middle one is a nonexistent binary — assert the other two contribute and no rejection. For sc-5-3: fire the AbortSignal after the first scanner completes and assert partial findings + child killed (process no longer running) within a bounded time. For sc-5-4: capture the fake client's prompt with and without scanners configured; assert the priors section is present/absent respectively and scannerRan flag matches; assert zero execa calls with scanners:[] (spy or injected runner). For sc-5-5: grep the new tests for any invocation of real 'slither'/'semgrep' binaries — none allowed. Full suite green.
